name: Cadalso to Descargamaría — circular via the GR 10 E03
name_original: Cadalso a Descargamaría — circular por el GR 10 E03
region_intro: Hiking around Cadalso offers a diverse landscape characterized by mountainous terrain, dense forests, and traditional villages within the Sierra de Gata region. The area features a network of trails that traverse varied elevations, including paths along the GR 10 E03 long-distance route. Hikers can expect to encounter a mix of challenging ascents and more gentle paths through natural surroundings. Best hiking trails around Cadalso The most popular hiking route is Torre Almenara from Gata — circular by Camino de la Almenara, a 6.1 miles (9.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 15 minutes to complete, featuring significant elevation gain towards the historic tower. Another top favourite among local hikers is Descargamaría to Ovejuela — circular via Robledillo de Gata, a difficult 13.7 miles (22.0 km) path, winding through rural landscapes and offering expansive views. Local hikers also love the Mirador de la Majá de la Sierra from Gato — circular by Praderas de el Pantano, a 12.3 miles (19.9 km) trail leading through mountain pastures and offering panoramic viewpoints, often completed in about 5 hours 59 minutes. Hiking around Cadalso is defined by mountainous paths, forested valleys, and historic village connections. The network offers options for easy hikes near Cadalso de los Vidrios, as well as moderate and difficult routes for varied ability levels. The routes in Cadalso are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Cadalso's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Cadalso?
A: Cadalso offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 20 trails available. These routes cater to different skill levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult options.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Cadalso?
A: The mountainous terrain of Cadalso, part of the Sierra de Gata region, is generally best explored during spring and autumn. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summer can be very hot, while winter may bring colder conditions and potential snow at higher elevations.

Q: Are there easy hikes suitable for beginners in Cadalso?
A: Yes, Cadalso has several easy hiking options perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. There are 6 easy routes available, offering gentle paths through the natural surroundings.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Cadalso?
A: Many of the trails in Cadalso are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14705839" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Stone Cross – Torre de Almenara loop from Gata&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate circular route that takes you through scenic landscapes.

Q: What interesting sights or landmarks can I see while hiking in Cadalso?
A: The Cadalso area is rich in natural beauty and historical points of interest. You can encounter landmarks such as the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4821468" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre de Almenara&lt;/a&gt;, charming settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5690813" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villasbuenas de Gata Village Square&lt;/a&gt;, and the picturesque village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6054062" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Robledillo de Gata&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to visit near Cadalso?
A: Yes, the region boasts beautiful natural features, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6053913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrituelo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which can be a refreshing stop on your hike.

Q: Where can I find scenic viewpoints on the trails?
A: Cadalso offers several trails with panoramic vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1272063273/mirador-de-la-maja-de-la-sierra-from-gato-circular-by-praderas-de-el-pantano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Majá de la Sierra from Gato&lt;/a&gt; route leads through mountain pastures to stunning viewpoints. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6968398" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorro de los Ángeles Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; provides expansive views of the landscape.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Cadalso?
A: While many trails involve varied elevations, there are easy routes that can be suitable for families. Look for shorter, less strenuous paths, particularly those labeled as 'easy' in the route descriptions, to ensure a comfortable experience for all ages.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Cadalso?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in the Cadalso area. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or protected wildlife. Please be mindful of local regulations and clean up after your pet.

Q: What are the parking options for trailheads in Cadalso?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. In many villages and near popular starting points, you can often find designated parking areas or roadside spots. It's recommended to check specific route details or local maps for the most convenient parking options for your chosen hike.

Q: Is it possible to reach trailheads by public transport?
A: Public transport options to reach specific trailheads in the Cadalso region can be limited, especially for more remote routes. While some villages may have bus connections, accessing the exact starting points of trails often requires a car. It's best to research local bus schedules if you plan to rely on public transport.

Q: Are there places to eat or get refreshments near the hiking paths?
A: Many hiking routes in Cadalso pass through or near traditional villages where you can find local cafes, bars, or small shops to refuel. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer routes, as services may not be directly on the trail.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Cadalso?
A: The hiking trails around Cadalso are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an impressive average rating of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the mix of challenging ascents and gentle paths, and the connection to historic villages.

Q: Are there any less crowded hiking trails in Cadalso?
A: To find less crowded trails, consider exploring some of the longer or more challenging routes, as these tend to attract fewer hikers. Additionally, hiking during off-peak seasons or on weekdays can offer a more solitary experience. The region has 21 trails, so there are plenty of options beyond the most popular ones.
tips: Natural pools on the side of the road for a quick dip. There is also a small park with a picnic area across the road.
Descargamaría is a municipality in the province of Cáceres. In 2011 it had a population of 190 inhabitants. An interesting fact is that it has the highest percentage of nudists in the world.
